rows.notes: Clear, centered year heading '1831.' very legible, no amounts present.

rows.description: Soluta - Dno. Rectoris. Scholaribus. et Afficiarijs &c.
rows.notes: Main page title under the year. Text is fairly clear but several Latin abbreviations and final word letters are slightly stylized (Afficiarijs/&c.), so transcription of minor letters is uncertain.
Left-margin section label beside the first bracket — 'Ad Fest. Nativ. 1830.' is clear and has no amounts.
Amounts align cleanly with the three vertical rules (19 | 13 | 4). Handwriting of description is legible; numbers pass currency validation.
Column alignment clear: 25 | 15 | 0. Place-name spellings (Nevington/Morton) are slightly uncertain from script but amounts are unambiguous.
Numbers read as 33 | 6 | 8 with clear vertical alignment. Surname 'Shiers' is legible though archaic spelling could vary.
Large round '60' in pounds column is very clear; shillings/pence are clearly '0'. Description readable as an augmentation relating to chaplains.
Repeated line 'Dno. Rectori' appears throughout the page; this instance reads clearly as 30 | 9 | 2 with good alignment.
Amount columns read clearly (89 | 3 | 8). The descriptive words are less clear (Div. Merlons./Merlons?); treated as an entry since amounts are present.
Second similarly formatted line with identical amounts (89 | 3 | 8). 'Id.' indicates same account; descriptive abbreviation clear enough to classify as entry.
Left-margin label introducing the next bracketed group; letters and the date '1831' are clear and no amounts on this line.
Pounds and shillings align (32 | 13). Pence appears to be '11' but the pence digit is slightly cramped/smudged; chosen '11' based on alignment with pence column and spacing.
Amounts read as 52 | 14 | 8 1/4. The small fraction mark after the '8' resembles a quarter (1/4); pounds/shillings clear. Place-name 'Hiddlington' reading moderately certain.
Standard 'Dno. Rectori' line repeated; amount 30 | 9 | 2 clearly aligned and legible.
Left-margin section label for the following group; 'D. Jo. Bapt.' (St. John the Baptist) and the year 1831 are clear.

rows.description: Stipend. ad Fest. D. Jo. Bapt. et Off.
rows.notes: Amounts read 17 | 13 | 4 with clear alignment. Description uses standard abbreviations; readable though slightly condensed.
Matches earlier 'Shiers' line: 33 | 6 | 8. Description abbreviated 'Id.' is clear; amounts unambiguous.

rows.description: Augm. comm. quoad Moder. et Sub D.
rows.notes: Pounds shown as clear '56' with 0 shillings/pence. Description contains abbreviations (Moder./Sub D.) but the monetary figures are unambiguous.

rows.description: Id. de Hiddlington pro 4to p. Anni
rows.notes: Amounts read as 17 | 11 | 6 3/4 (the small fraction mark after the '6' closely matches three-quarters). Pounds/shillings alignment is clear; fraction legible though small.
Another 'Dno. Rectori' repetition with clear amounts 30 | 9 | 2. Very consistent with other occurrences on the page.
Left-margin label for final bracket. 'J. Mich.' (St. Michael) and '1831' are clearly written; no amounts on this line.

rows.description: Stipend Quinquenear ad J. S. Mich.
rows.notes: Amounts read as 23 | 15 | 0 with clear alignment. The descriptive heading is somewhat abbreviated ('Quinquenear' inferred from script), so description certainty is moderate.

Numeric columns read as 38 | 7 | 5 1/4 — the small quarter mark after the '5' is faint but consistent with earlier quarter marks. The place-name 'Whittlesham' is partly ambiguous in letters, reducing confidence.
Final repetition of 'Dno. Rectori' with clear amounts 30 | 9 | 2. High confidence due to identical earlier occurrences and good alignment.

Final total shown at lower right: pounds read as '411', shillings '1', pence '0' and a small quarter mark to the right interpreted as 1/4 (0.25). Fraction is small but located where other quarter marks appear; alignment of main numbers is clear.
